literal: vibration, throb, subtle pulsation; from √spand 'to throb'
invariant_definition: Vibration — Trika's term for the constitutive pulsation of Śiva-consciousness, the dynamic aspect of the unified *prakāśa-vimarśa*. *Spanda* is the metaphysical principle that the supreme is not static but constitutively pulsating; the world is the rhythm of this pulsation. Vasugupta's *Spanda-Kārikā* (or his disciple Kallaṭa's; the authorship is disputed) is the foundational text. The *spanda*-doctrine is what allows Trika to be a non-dualism without being a static-monism.

per_school.definition: *Spanda* is the subtle vibration that is Śiva-Śakti's constitutive activity. Distinguished from gross motion (which is in time) — *spanda* is timeless yet dynamic, the rhythm of the eternal. Recognition of one's own consciousness as *spanda* is one mode of *pratyabhijñā*.

translator_note: 'Vibration' or 'pulsation' acceptable. Western readers should not import Bergson's *durée* (although there is structural resemblance) — *spanda* is metaphysical-cosmological, not phenomenological-temporal.
